    "R1.2": "The abstract contains a detectable internal inconsistency: it simultaneously claims non-smooth losses, O(1/T) acceleration, and then states Theorem 1 under L-Lipschitz gradient (smoothness) yielding O(1/sqrt(T)) — a logical contradiction that a technical reviewer would flag as load-bearing.",
    "R1.3": "The sole empirical datum (30% fewer epochs on CIFAR-10 at coverage 0.7) is offered to support a broad 'first-of-kind' claim; whether this alignment is adequate is a natural dimension of 'what do you think' on a research artifact.",

rationale.R1.1: Delegation invites open-ended assessment; the contribution claim (SelectMD as first mirror-descent method with accelerated convergence on non-smooth selective losses) is a natural target, but it is unclear whether evaluating the thesis is the primary ask.
rationale.R1.2: The abstract contains a detectable internal inconsistency: it simultaneously claims non-smooth losses, O(1/T) acceleration, and then states Theorem 1 under L-Lipschitz gradient (smoothness) yielding O(1/sqrt(T)) — a logical contradiction that a technical reviewer would flag as load-bearing.
rationale.R1.3: The sole empirical datum (30% fewer epochs on CIFAR-10 at coverage 0.7) is offered to support a broad 'first-of-kind' claim; whether this alignment is adequate is a natural dimension of 'what do you think' on a research artifact.
rationale.R1.4: The 'first mirror-descent procedure' novelty claim is prominent and directly falsifiable against prior selective-risk and mirror-descent literature; a general opinion would be expected to touch this.

parsed.clarification_question: Are you asking primarily for a mathematical and logical correctness review (verifying theorem conditions, rate claims, and internal consistency), rather than an assessment of novelty and contribution positioning relative to prior work?
